review_execution.command: claude -p --model claude-opus-4-8 --effort max --permission-mode dontAsk --tools "" --output-format json < staged diff prompt > /tmp/cp00-102-claude-review-output-valid-02.json

parsed_review.rationale: Reviewed only the staged diff (no tools used). The change adds CP00-102 as a reference-only, synthetic-only permission matrix and security interaction catalog and meets every scope constraint. (1) Synthetic-only: every matrix row and interaction sets synthetic_only:true and uses_real_client_data:false; createCoreDomainCp102SecurityInteractionSet sources IDs only from createCoreDomainSyntheticFixtureSet using clearly synthetic prefixes (req_rp01_cp102_security, perm_rp01_cp102_reference, audit_ref_rp01_cp102_hint), and manifest/evidence carry no_real_data:true and synthetic_only:true. (2) Reference-only / no runtime permission evaluation: rows carry permission_reference_only:true and evaluates_runtime_permission:false; permission_policy is reference_matrix_only_until_rp02_permission_kernel; deny_over_allow_policy is deny_reference_wins_in_static_matrix; security_trimming_policy is expected_omission_proof_only_no_runtime_filtering. No code path evaluates permissions at runtime — all functions only construct frozen static objects. (3) No audit ledger writes: writes_audit_event:false everywhere, audit_hint_only:true on rows, audit_event_expectation interaction is audit_expectation_only:true, and audit_policy is audit_hint_expectation_only_until_rp03_audit_kernel; audit_event_id/audit_hint_fields are reference strings/field-name lists, not write operations. (4) No product state writes / (5) no database rows: writes_product_state:false and creates_database_rows:false on the contract, every row, every interaction, and the manifest no_write_attestation; no I/O, persistence, or mutation occurs. (6) No real client data: confirmed via uses_real_client_data:false and accepts_real_client_data:false. (7) No LDIP implementation: ldip_implemented:false on contract, rows, interactions, and handoff; handoff hrx_boundary documents the embedded-evidence boundary. (8) Production_ready gating is adequate: validateCoreDomainCp102Coverage enforces 6 action bindings, 10 interaction bindings, 11 micro-phases, 150 covered units, exactly 7 rows and 10 interactions, presence of the deny-over-allow reference row and a security-trimming omission proof, and a loop asserting synthetic-only + all no-write/non-runtime/non-LDIP flags across every row and interaction; the JS contract and the JSON permission_matrix_contract both pin all six no-write booleans to false; the validator (validate-rp01-core-domain-contract.mjs) cross-checks the JSON contract pack id, range, upstream linkage, action/interaction/micro-phase/forbidden-claim membership, all no-write booleans, and the four policies, and gates current_pack.production_ready_flag = core_domain_permission_matrix_reference_catalog_verified plus cp102Coverage.valid===true and the next_subphase RP01.P06.M08.S17; tests assert the same coverage, frozen immutability, and the Hermes evidence command_anchors (including npm run closeout-pack:validate CP00-102). Index re-export and all imported symbols are present and consistent. No P0/P1/P2/P3 issues identified; the staged changes are safe and within the declared no-write, reference-only, synthetic-only envelope.
notes: No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings were reported by the valid CP00-102 pack-level Claude review.
The invalid first attempt is preserved for auditability and was not counted as the valid pack-level review.
